_meta.dna: performance(excellent) = speed(sub_second) x memory(bounded) x battery(efficient) x network(minimal) x render(smooth_60fps) x startup(instant) x scale(graceful)
_meta.question_expansion_instruction: Each floor lists 7 seed questions. During execution, expand to ~100 questions per persona. Performance is the silent feature — users don't notice when it's good, but they leave when it's bad. Every question must be answerable by measuring actual metrics in solace-browser.

name: Tower of Performance
protocol: FORECAST -> CROSS-REF -> AUDIT -> FIX -> VERIFY -> SEAL -> POSTMORTEM

questions: What is the Largest Contentful Paint (LCP) for the home page — is it under 2.5s?
What is the First Input Delay (FID) or Interaction to Next Paint (INP) on interactive pages?
What is the Cumulative Layout Shift (CLS) — are there any layout jumps above 0.1?
Does the theme switching cause a visible layout shift (CLS regression)?
Are critical CSS and fonts preloaded to minimize LCP?
Does the FOUC prevention in layout.js actually prevent flash of unstyled content?
Are there any render-blocking resources that delay First Contentful Paint?
What is the total JavaScript bundle size across all pages?
Are scripts deferred or async where appropriate to avoid blocking the main thread?
Is there any JavaScript that runs for more than 50ms blocking the main thread (long tasks)?
Are there memory leaks in long-running JavaScript (event listeners not cleaned up)?
Does the SolaceTheme IIFE add measurable overhead to page load?
Are unused JavaScript functions tree-shaken or dead code eliminated?
Is there code splitting for pages that don't need all scripts loaded upfront?
What is the total CSS bundle size (site.css + theme CSS)?
Are there unused CSS rules that increase parse time without styling anything?
Do the ~60 CSS custom properties in each theme cause measurable performance impact?
Is CSS loaded in the optimal order (critical above fold, rest deferred)?
Are there expensive CSS selectors (deep nesting, universal selectors, attribute selectors)?
Does the theme hot-swap (link element replacement) cause a render flash?
Are CSS animations GPU-accelerated (transform/opacity) or causing layout thrashing?
How many HTTP requests does the home page make on first load?
Is HTTP/2 or HTTP/3 being used for multiplexing requests?
Are static assets cached with appropriate Cache-Control headers?
Is gzip or brotli compression enabled for text responses?
Are images optimized (WebP, proper sizing, lazy loading)?
Does the local HTTP server (http_server.js) set proper caching headers?
Are there unnecessary network requests on page load (analytics, fonts, polyfills)?
How long does it take from double-clicking the app icon to seeing the home page?
Is the Electron/Playwright browser engine startup time acceptable (<3s)?
Does the Python backend (http_server.js or Python process) start before the browser window?
Are there expensive initialization operations that could be deferred to after first paint?
Does the app show a splash screen or loading state during startup?
Is there a warm-start optimization for subsequent app launches?
Does the first-run experience (OOBE) add significant startup overhead?
What is the baseline memory usage of Solace Browser at idle?
Does memory usage grow unboundedly over time (memory leak)?
Are DOM nodes properly cleaned up when navigating between pages?
Does each browser tab/session have bounded memory usage?
Are large data structures (evidence chains, recipes) lazy-loaded?
Does the browser process memory stay under 500MB during normal use?
Are event listeners and interval timers cleaned up when views change?
Does the browser use CPU efficiently when idle (no busy-wait loops)?
Are animations paused when the app is in background or minimized?
Does the daemon scheduler use efficient sleep patterns (not polling)?
Are network requests batched to minimize radio wake-ups on laptops?
Does the midnight theme (true black) actually help OLED battery life?
Are there WebSocket connections that keep the network interface active unnecessarily?
Does the browser reduce activity when on battery power?
Are there forced synchronous layouts (layout thrashing) in the JavaScript code?
Does scrolling maintain 60fps on content-heavy pages (schedule, app store)?
Are CSS transitions hardware-accelerated with will-change or transform3d hints?
Does the bottom rail (YinYang) animation affect scroll performance?
Are there paint storms when interacting with the settings page theme editor?
Does the header/footer injection in layout.js cause unnecessary repaints?
Are SVG icons and illustrations rendered efficiently (not rasterized at wrong size)?
Are web fonts loaded with font-display: swap to prevent invisible text?
How many font files are loaded and what is their total size?
Are fonts preloaded in the document head?
Is there a system font stack fallback that provides good appearance during loading?
Are font subsets used (Latin only if no CJK needed per locale)?
Does font loading cause layout shifts (CLS) when fonts swap in?
Are variable fonts used to reduce the number of font files?
Are images in modern formats (WebP, AVIF) with fallbacks for older browsers?
Are images lazy-loaded below the fold?
Are image dimensions specified to prevent layout shift?
Are there any images larger than 500KB that could be optimized?
Are SVGs used for icons and illustrations instead of raster images?
Does the app store use placeholder images while thumbnails load?
Are responsive images (srcset) used for different viewport sizes?
What is the p50/p95/p99 response time for the /api/locale endpoint?
Are API responses cached where appropriate to reduce latency?
Does the local HTTP server handle concurrent requests efficiently?
Are there any API endpoints that take more than 500ms to respond?
Is there request queuing or backpressure when the server is under load?
Does the browser make unnecessary API calls on page navigation?
Are API responses minimized (no extra fields, no verbose formatting)?
Is there a service worker providing offline capability?
Are static assets cached in a service worker cache for instant loading?
Does the service worker use a stale-while-revalidate strategy for dynamic content?
Can the app function offline (at least showing cached pages)?
Does the service worker cache invalidation work correctly on app updates?
Is the service worker scope configured correctly for all routes?
Does the service worker interfere with the theme switching mechanism?
Does the evidence chain (append-only) cause excessive disk I/O?
Are config files (settings, theme preferences) read efficiently with caching?
Does the recipe storage use efficient serialization (not pretty-printed JSON)?
Are file system watches (hot reload) efficient or causing excessive CPU?
Does the app store data cache locally to avoid re-reading from disk?
Are temporary files cleaned up to prevent disk space accumulation?
Does the vault (encrypted token store) use efficient I/O patterns?
Does the Python backend use async I/O efficiently for concurrent requests?
Are browser automation tasks parallelized where possible?
Does the daemon scheduler handle concurrent task execution safely?
Are there race conditions in the evidence chain (concurrent writes)?
Does the local server handle WebSocket connections without blocking HTTP?
Are CPU-intensive operations offloaded to worker threads?
Is the event loop (Node.js or Python asyncio) kept responsive?
Are frequently accessed records indexed for fast lookup?
Does the local data store (JSON files) scale with number of apps/recipes?
Are there N+1 query patterns (loading list then each item individually)?
Is pagination implemented for large data sets (recipes, evidence)?
Are data lookups cached in memory for hot paths?
Does the oracle memory JSON grow unboundedly?
Are search operations (memory search, recipe search) efficient?
What is the total installed dependency count and size?
Are there large dependencies that could be replaced with lighter alternatives?
Is tree-shaking working for ES module imports?
Are polyfills loaded conditionally based on browser support?
Does the build process minify JavaScript and CSS for production?
Are source maps generated for development but excluded from production?
Are there duplicate dependencies in the dependency tree?
Does the app store listing page maintain 60fps while scrolling through many apps?
Is virtual scrolling (windowing) used for long lists?
Are scroll event handlers debounced or using requestAnimationFrame?
Does the schedule view handle 100+ scheduled tasks without jank?
Are fixed/sticky elements (header, footer, bottom rail) GPU-composited?
Does infinite scroll (if used) trigger at the right threshold?
Are heavy DOM operations batched to avoid layout thrashing during scroll?
Do all animations use transform and opacity (composite-only properties)?
Are animations disabled when prefers-reduced-motion is set?
Does the theme toggle rotation animation cause paint storms?
Are JavaScript animations using requestAnimationFrame (not setInterval)?
Do loading spinners and skeleton screens perform well on low-end devices?
Are animation durations appropriate (not too long, not too short)?
Are there animations that trigger on every scroll event?
Do ARIA attributes cause measurable performance overhead?
Does the focus management (skip-nav, focus-visible) work without jank?
Are screen reader announcements (aria-live) non-blocking?
Does high contrast mode (prefers-contrast: more) render as fast as standard mode?
Are keyboard event handlers efficient (not doing heavy work on every keypress)?
Does the forced-colors mode cause rendering differences that affect performance?
Are accessible tooltips and popovers rendered lazily?
How large are the locale JSON files and does loading them affect page start?
Is the locale file cached after first load (lru_cache in i18n.py)?
Does switching locale cause a full page reload or efficient DOM update?
Are locale files lazy-loaded (only the active locale, not all 23)?
Does RTL layout switching cause a visible re-render?
Are translation lookups (t() function) fast enough for real-time use?
Does the i18n system handle missing keys without throwing errors that slow rendering?
How long does a full build take from clean checkout to production artifact?
Is there hot module replacement (HMR) for development iteration speed?
Does the CI/CD pipeline cache dependencies between builds?
Are tests parallelized in CI for faster feedback?
Is the Docker image (if used) optimized for layer caching?
Does the build process use incremental compilation?
Are there unnecessary build steps that could be eliminated?
Is there real user monitoring (RUM) capturing actual user performance data?
Are performance metrics (LCP, FID, CLS) reported to an analytics endpoint?
Is there a performance budget that triggers alerts when exceeded?
Can performance data be segmented by device type, network, and locale?
Are slow-loading pages identified automatically from RUM data?
Is there A/B testing infrastructure for measuring performance impact of changes?
Are performance regression tests part of the CI pipeline?
Does the browser work acceptably on a 5-year-old laptop with 4GB RAM?
Is there a lite mode for low-end devices?
Does the app detect device capabilities and adapt accordingly?
Are there fallbacks for devices that don't support modern CSS features?
Does the app work on slow networks (3G, high latency)?
Is there progressive enhancement so core features work without JavaScript?
Does the app degrade gracefully when resources are constrained?
Does CSP header parsing cause measurable performance overhead?
Is CORS preflight causing unnecessary round-trips?
Does OAuth3 token validation add latency to authenticated requests?
Is the vault decryption (AES-256-GCM) fast enough for responsive UX?
Does the evidence hash chain (SHA-256) cause noticeable latency?
Are security headers cached to avoid re-computation?
Does TLS handshake overhead affect API response times?
What is the memory overhead of the Electron/Playwright wrapper vs raw Chromium?
Does IPC between main and renderer processes cause latency?
Are there unnecessary electron preload scripts that slow startup?
Does the native menu/tray use memory efficiently?
Is the app using hardware acceleration where available?
Does the auto-updater mechanism cause background resource consumption?
Are there native node modules that need compilation and cause cold start delay?

floors.persona_domain: performance of recipe execution and replay
questions: How fast is recipe replay vs first-time LLM execution?
Does recipe caching use memory efficiently (not loading all 81+ recipes at startup)?
Is recipe matching (finding the right cached recipe) O(1) or O(n)?
Does recipe serialization/deserialization add measurable overhead?
Are large recipe outputs streamed or loaded entirely into memory?
Does the recipe hit rate metric tracking cause performance overhead?
Can recipe replay execute in under 100ms for simple operations?
Does evidence chain append-only writing scale with chain length?
Is evidence hashing (SHA-256) done synchronously on the main thread?
Does the evidence file grow unboundedly over time?
Is evidence verification (integrity check) fast enough for real-time use?
Are evidence files compacted or archived after a threshold?
Does evidence capture add latency to user-facing operations?
Can evidence queries (search, filter by date) perform well on large chains?
Are WebSocket connections used efficiently (shared, not per-feature)?
Is there heartbeat/keepalive overhead from WebSocket connections?
Does WebSocket reconnection handle network changes gracefully?
Are WebSocket messages batched to reduce frame overhead?
Is there flow control for high-frequency WebSocket messages?
Does the WebSocket implementation handle backpressure?
Are binary WebSocket frames used for efficiency where appropriate?
How fast is the memory search (TF-IDF) for typical queries?
Is the search index built lazily or at startup?
Does the app store search return results in under 200ms?
Is there typeahead/autocomplete performance optimization?
Are search results paginated for large result sets?
Does the search algorithm handle 10,000+ indexed items efficiently?
Is the search index updated incrementally (not rebuilt from scratch)?
Do CJK locales (Chinese, Japanese, Korean) render at the same speed as Latin?
Does Arabic/Hebrew RTL layout cause measurable render overhead?
Are locale-specific fonts loaded lazily based on the active locale?
Does the locale switcher re-render the page efficiently?
Are there performance differences between the 23 supported locales?
Does bi-directional text (mixed LTR/RTL) cause layout performance issues?
Are locale JSON files appropriately sized (not bloated with unused keys)?
How fast can Solace Browser navigate to a page and extract content?
Does browser automation add overhead vs manual browsing?
Can automation tasks run in parallel without degrading UI performance?
Is the Playwright automation layer efficient with memory?
Does screenshot capture cause visible UI stutter?
Are automation wait strategies (wait for selector, wait for network) efficient?
Does automated form filling perform at human-like speed for site compatibility?
Does localStorage/sessionStorage access block the main thread?
Are there performance issues with large localStorage entries?
Is IndexedDB used for structured data that exceeds localStorage limits?
Does file system I/O (config, recipes, evidence) use async patterns?
Are JSON parse/stringify operations causing main thread jank for large objects?
Is there a data access layer that batches writes?
Does the vault (encrypted storage) use efficient I/O patterns?
Is startup time consistent across Linux, macOS, and Windows?
Are there OS-specific performance bottlenecks (e.g., Windows antivirus scanning)?
Does the macOS universal binary (ARM + x86) perform equally on both architectures?
Are there file system performance differences across operating systems?
Does the app handle different display densities (Retina, HiDPI) efficiently?
Are there OS-specific memory management differences?
Does the app integrate with OS-level performance APIs (Activity Monitor, Task Manager)?
Do error handling paths (try/catch, error boundaries) cause performance overhead?
Is error logging asynchronous or blocking?
Do frequent console.warn/console.error calls affect performance in production?
Are error recovery paths optimized (not just correct)?
Does error reporting to external services happen asynchronously?
Are there error loops (error → retry → error) that consume resources?
Is the Fallback Ban (no silent swallowing) compatible with performance goals?
Are above-the-fold elements loaded first on every page?
Is there a skeleton screen or loading state for heavy components?
Are below-the-fold images and components lazy-loaded?
Does the app store load app listings incrementally as the user scrolls?
Are third-party resources (if any) loaded asynchronously?
Is there a content priority system that loads critical content first?
Does progressive loading work correctly on slow networks?
Does theme switching (dark→light→midnight) happen in under 50ms?
Does the theme CSS file swap cause a visible flash?
Are CSS custom property changes batched or do they trigger per-property repaints?
Does the custom theme editor real-time preview perform smoothly?
Does localStorage theme persistence add measurable startup latency?
Are theme-specific resources (if any) preloaded or loaded on demand?
Does the theme toggle button animation perform at 60fps?
What is the requests/second capacity of the local HTTP server?
Does the server handle keep-alive connections efficiently?
Is static file serving optimized with sendfile or mmap?
Does the server support HTTP/2 for multiplexing?
Are there request queuing bottlenecks under concurrent load?
Does the server use efficient buffering for large responses?
Is the server startup time minimal (under 500ms)?
Are browser tabs/sessions properly cleaned up when closed?
Does the app release file handles after reading/writing?
Are temporary files cleaned up on app exit?
Does the garbage collector cause visible pauses (GC jank)?
Are large objects (screenshots, evidence) released after use?
Is there a resource monitoring system that detects leaks?
Does the app clean up network connections on shutdown?
Are likely-next-page resources preloaded based on user behavior?
Does the app prefetch data for pages the user is likely to visit?
Are DNS prefetch hints used for external domains?
Is there preconnect for frequently used API endpoints?
Does the app avoid over-preloading that wastes bandwidth?
Are preloaded resources used within a reasonable time (not wasted)?
Is there a preloading strategy that adapts to network conditions?
Is there a performance dashboard visible to developers?
Are key metrics (memory, CPU, network) tracked in real-time?
Can users see how much memory/CPU the browser is using?
Are there performance alerts for anomalous behavior?
Is performance data logged for trend analysis?
Can the machine dashboard show per-tab resource usage?
Are there performance comparison tools (before/after change)?
Is there an automated performance test suite that runs in CI?
Are performance baselines established and tracked over time?
Does the benchmark suite test all critical user journeys?
Are benchmarks run on representative hardware (not just dev machines)?
Is there a performance regression gate in the CI pipeline?
Are benchmark results stored as evidence for tracking?
Does the benchmark suite cover both startup and steady-state performance?
Do JSON data files (recipes, evidence, oracle) grow without bounds?
Is there a compaction or rotation strategy for large data files?
Does the oracle-memory.json file scale with increasing audits?
Are old evidence entries archived or pruned?
Is there disk space monitoring that alerts when storage is low?
Does the app handle 1GB+ data directories without performance issues?
Are data migrations efficient when updating file formats?
Do API calls to solaceagi.com have appropriate timeouts?
Are external service calls non-blocking (don't freeze the UI)?
Is there circuit breaking for flaky external services?
Are responses from external services cached appropriately?
Does the LLM proxy (Together.ai/OpenRouter) add measurable latency?
Are there retry storms when external services are slow?
Do failed external calls degrade gracefully without slowing the app?
Are touch interactions responsive (under 100ms feedback)?
Does the app handle touch and mouse events efficiently (no double-firing)?
Are gesture recognizers (swipe, pinch) performant?
Does scrolling work smoothly on touch devices?
Are touch targets large enough (44px) without causing layout overhead?
Does the app avoid delay on touch events (no 300ms click delay)?
Are there passive event listeners where appropriate for scroll performance?
Does the print stylesheet load efficiently when printing?
Are evidence reports printable without excessive rendering time?
Does PDF export (if available) complete in reasonable time?
Are print styles cached to avoid re-computation?
Does the print view handle large evidence chains?
Are non-print elements hidden efficiently (not just visibility:hidden)?
Does print preview render correctly across browsers?
Does the auto-updater download efficiently (delta updates, not full binary)?
Is the update check non-blocking (doesn't slow normal operation)?
Does the update installation complete quickly (under 30 seconds)?
Can the app continue operating while downloading an update?
Are update artifacts cached to avoid re-downloading on failure?
Does the update process preserve user data and settings?
Is the update verification (checksum, signature) fast?
What happens when 50+ browser tabs are open simultaneously?
Does the app remain responsive when processing a 10,000-entry evidence chain?
What is the behavior with 1000+ recipes loaded?
Does the app handle running out of disk space gracefully?
What happens when network bandwidth drops to near-zero?
Can the daemon scheduler handle 100+ concurrent scheduled tasks?
Does the app recover gracefully after being killed and restarted?
Does the browser feel fast — is the perceived performance excellent?
Is the performance budget documented and enforced?
Does performance improve over time (not just not-regress)?
Is the recipe replay model delivering on the cost-reduction promise through performance?
Would a power user choose Solace Browser over Chrome for daily use based on speed?
Is the performance testing infrastructure as strong as the security testing?
Does the Dragon Rider feel proud of the browser's performance?
Does the browser feel snappy to a non-technical user?
Are there any moments where a non-technical user would think 'this is slow'?
Does the browser start up fast enough that the user doesn't switch to Chrome?
Are loading states clear (the user knows something is happening, not frozen)?
Does the browser work well on the user's existing hardware (not just developer machines)?
Is the battery drain noticeable compared to regular Chrome?
Would a non-technical user say 'this feels smooth' after a week of daily use?
